metadata.source: BNF, WHO, CDSCO Drug Prescribing in Renal and Hepatic Failure guidelines

contraindications.reason: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis causing vasoconstriction and acute kidney injury. Also worsen hypertension, heart failure, and GI mucosal integrity.
contraindications.alternative: Paracetamol for analgesia; proton pump inhibitors if GI risk present

contraindications.reason: Impaired drug clearance leads to accumulation and potentially fatal lactic acidosis.
contraindications.alternative: SGLT2 inhibitors (if eGFR >45), GLP-1 agonists, or insulin

contraindications.reason: ACE inhibitors cause severe fetal renal toxicity (pregnancy) and acute renal failure in bilateral RAS.
contraindications.alternative: Nifedipine/amlodipine for hypertension in pregnancy; specialist review for RAS

contraindications.reason: Fluoroquinolones lower seizure threshold and can exacerbate myasthenia gravis by blocking neuromuscular junction.
contraindications.alternative: Use alternative antibiotic class based on susceptibility testing

contraindications.reason: Statins are hepatically metabolized and can worsen hepatic function in active liver disease.
contraindications.alternative: Treat underlying liver disease first; reassess cardiovascular risk

contraindications.reason: Corticosteroids worsen glycaemic control, accelerate bone loss, suppress immunity worsening infections, and damage GI mucosa.
contraindications.alternative: Use steroid-sparing immunosuppressants where possible; add bone protection and blood sugar monitoring

contraindications.reason: Anticoagulants increase bleeding risk. Active hemorrhage or recent intracranial bleed is an absolute contraindication.
contraindications.alternative: Treat underlying bleeding source first. Use mechanical prophylaxis if anticoagulation urgently needed.

contraindications.reason: Beta-blockers cause bronchospasm in asthma/COPD and worsen cardiac conduction blocks.
contraindications.alternative: Selective beta-1 blockers (bisoprolol) with caution in mild COPD; CCBs for HR control; pacemaker for blocks

contraindications.reason: Tetracyclines deposit in fetal bone and teeth causing permanent discoloration and bone growth impairment.
contraindications.alternative: Azithromycin or amoxicillin for most indications in pregnancy

contraindications.reason: Thiazolidinediones cause fluid retention worsening heart failure. Pioglitazone associated with bladder cancer risk.
contraindications.alternative: Metformin, SGLT2 inhibitors, or GLP-1 agonists

contraindications.reason: Digoxin increases contractility which worsens HOCM outflow obstruction. In WPW, it can enhance accessory pathway conduction causing VF.
contraindications.alternative: Beta-blockers or calcium channel blockers for rate control

contraindications.reason: Aminoglycosides are nephrotoxic and ototoxic. Accumulate in renal impairment. Worsen neuromuscular blockade in myasthenia.
contraindications.alternative: Use alternative antibiotics if available; if essential, dose adjust for renal function and monitor drug levels

contraindications.reason: Active opioid metabolites accumulate in renal failure causing prolonged CNS/respiratory depression. Opioids suppress respiratory drive worsening COPD/asthma.
contraindications.alternative: Fentanyl (safer in renal failure). Low-dose with monitoring. Non-opioid analgesics preferred.

contraindications.reason: Sulfonylureas cause prolonged hypoglycaemia in renal/hepatic failure due to drug/metabolite accumulation. High-risk in elderly.
contraindications.alternative: Gliclazide MR preferred in mild-moderate CKD. Insulin for severe impairment.

contraindications.reason: Aldosterone antagonists reduce potassium excretion. In hyperkalemia or renal failure, can cause fatal cardiac arrest.
contraindications.alternative: Loop diuretics. Patiromer or sodium zirconium cyclosilicate for K+ management.
